required_skills: Satellite systems architecture design including bus and payload subsystem integration
Mission analysis and orbit design including LEO, MEO, GEO, and HEO trajectory selection
Link budget analysis for RF communications and telemetry, tracking, and command (TT&C) systems
Power system design including solar array sizing, battery selection, and power budget management
Thermal control system design including passive and active thermal management techniques
Model-based systems engineering (MBSE) and requirements management using SysML
Mass, power, and data budget management across satellite subsystems
Integration and test (I&T) planning for satellite assemblies in cleanroom environments
Launch vehicle interface management and separation system design
System-level FMEA, fault tree analysis (FTA), and reliability modeling for on-orbit operations
preferred_skills: Experience with small satellite (SmallSat, CubeSat, or microsatellite) mission development
Knowledge of software-defined radio (SDR) and adaptive communications payload architectures
Familiarity with space debris mitigation guidelines and IADC disposal orbit standards
Experience with on-orbit servicing, assembly, and manufacturing (OSAM) concepts
Knowledge of radiation environment modeling and total ionizing dose (TID) analysis
Familiarity with NASA GEVS or ECSS environmental test standards for space qualification
Experience with ground segment design and mission operations center (MOC) development
responsibilities: Define and maintain satellite system architecture by allocating mission requirements to bus and payload subsystems and managing interface control documents (ICDs) across all subsystem teams
Perform mission analysis and orbit trade studies using STK and GMAT to optimize ground coverage, revisit rates, launch windows, and delta-V budgets for station-keeping and disposal
Develop and manage satellite mass, power, data rate, and link budgets across all mission phases from launch and early orbit through end-of-life disposal
Lead system-level integration and test campaigns in cleanroom environments, coordinating functional, environmental, and RF testing of satellite flight hardware
Conduct RF link budget analyses for TT&C and mission data downlink chains, specifying antenna configurations, EIRP, G/T, and margin against rain fade and interference scenarios
Design and verify satellite thermal control architectures using Thermal Desktop simulations, selecting radiators, MLI blankets, heaters, and heat pipes to meet component temperature limits
Execute FMEA, FTA, and reliability block diagram (RBD) analyses to identify single-point failures and drive redundancy and fault management design decisions
Manage launch vehicle interface requirements including payload fairing constraints, separation system qualification, and mechanical and electrical interface verification
Support satellite CDR, ship review, and launch readiness review (LRR) by preparing compliance matrices, anomaly resolution reports, and as-built configuration documentation
Collaborate with mission operations teams to develop on-orbit commissioning plans, anomaly response procedures, and end-of-life decommissioning sequences
